ops.title: capt-hook: absorb cc-transcript 14.5 async persistence inversion, lift pin to <15
ops.description: capt-hook 10.5.0 pins cc-transcript[judge]>=14.4,<14.5 deliberately: 14.5.0 flipped the whole persistence tier to native-async (reversing 14.2's sync ruling) — `await FeedbackStore.open(...)`, async methods everywhere, `async with store.transaction()`, judge.similar's seven store-touching functions async again, record_verdict async again, CorrectionLog/DecisionLog/HeartbeatLog/Watcher async, AsyncLedger. capt-hook's ReviewStore composition (commit c0df3e77, sync per 14.2-14.4 design) breaks with `'coroutine' object has no attribute 'sql'` against >=14.5. The absorption = async-ify every store call site (review/store.py, scan.py, judge.py, notify/announce paths, tests) per the 14.5.0 changelog map, then lift the pin to <15. Note 14.6.1 also added a persist lock in judge.run_verdicts worth mirroring. Beware continued upstream churn — re-read the cc-transcript changelog at execution start.

ops.text: Full capt-hook suite green under uv run against the latest cc-transcript 14.x with pin >=14.5-compatible floor and <15 ceiling
Review subsystem verified end-to-end (scan, judge, announce) against the async store
Pin lifted in the same release that ships the absorption
